title: ophrys tenthredinifera proles neglecta
intro: Ophrys tenthredinifera proles neglecta, also known as the sawfly orchid, is a subspecies of the Ophrys tenthredinifera plant, a type of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. The health benefits of the sawfly orchid include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and even have antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in natural medicine. The herbal preparations of the sawfly orchid include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
